LINZ To MOSCOW, IDA.

pages.raw_text:
The chamber of commerce has
be held Tuesday night for the pur-
~ose of endorsing the plan to build
- new electric interurban line from
pokane to Moscow. Idaho. via Col
AK The plan is submitted by the
trustees of the new company, jay +.
Graves, +. A. Blackwell, Alfred Cool
idge. f. Lewis Clark and j. c. Twohy.


At a meeting of business men and
capitalists held in the Old National
bank the following resolution was
adopted:


Resolved, That we, merchants,
bankers, jobbers and manufacturers
of Spokane, endorse the project Of
building an electric railroad from
Spokane to Moscow. Idaho, and that
we urge the Spokane chamber Of
commerce to call a meeting OF the
citizens of Spokane at the chamber
of commerce rooms, next to the Sill
Yer Grill, at y. m. Tuesday, January
10, to fully discuss the needs of such
n road; the benefits that Will accrue
TO Spokane merchants by bringing
the richest agricultural section Of the
Inland Empire into close touch with
this city's business interests, and to
determine how this enterprise can be
successfully accomplished"


Speeches were made by a number Of
capitalists and business men favoring
the new enterprise and pledges of
support given The novel idea of
stock subscriptions is given in the
following by Jay 7. Graves:


Our proposal to the public is that
we think ii is most desirable that
this road should be owned and con-
trolled by home people-namely east
